content.heading: Overview
content.body: An exclusive members' lounge for a private club catering to Kampala's business and diplomatic community. The space needed to function as both a working environment and a social one — somewhere a member could take a discreet call or host an informal dinner within the same visit.

items.caption: The main lounge — layered seating zones for different group sizes.

items.heading: Atmosphere
items.body: Dark walls in a deep charcoal-green, leather club chairs, and low ambient lighting create a sense of enclosure and privacy. The space reads as serious without being cold — warm metals, layered textiles, and a curated art selection loosen the formality just enough.

content.caption: One of four semi-private dining alcoves, bookable by members for small gatherings.

content.heading: Spatial Planning
content.body: The 380 sqm floor plate was divided into five distinct zones: entry and cloaking, main lounge, working bays, bar and casual dining, and private meeting rooms. Despite the complexity, the flow through the space feels intuitive and unhurried.
